channel.title: 2026-05-16 AI × Science 文献日报 RSS
channel.description: 2026-05-16 当天筛选出的 AI × 物理 / 化学 / 材料交叉文献 RSS。

item.title: Tunable Distance Quantum Error Correction at 29 Percent Encoding Rate of the Face Centered Cubic Lattice
item.description:
📚 Preprints
👤 Raghu Kulkarni
The [[3𝐿3, 2𝐿3+2, 3]] CSS code on the Face-Centered Cubic (FCC) lattice achieves 67.7% encoding rate with weight-12 stabilizers, but its distance is capped at 𝑑 = 3 by weight-3 logical operators sitting at tetrahedral interstitial voids. These logicals carry a geometric fingerprint—one error per triad sheet, all three converging at a known void—that makes them detectable by a flag ancilla measuring the three-body parity 𝑍𝑒𝑎𝑍𝑒𝑏𝑍𝑒𝑐 . The weight-3 logical flips this parity from +1 to −1; the stabilizer syndrome stays silent, but the flag sees it. Running 𝑅 independent flag rounds per syndrome cycle pushes the logical failure rate to 𝑝3+𝑅, so the effective distance is simply 𝑑eff = 3+𝑅—adjustable at runtime, with no changes to the code or its logical count. At 𝑅 = 4 the FCC+flags code achieve…

item.title: Linking DC transport properties and entanglement negativity in non-Hermitian superlattices
item.description:
📚 Preprints
👤 L. S. Lima
We investigate how non-Hermitian effects in one-dimensional superlattices incorporating gain and loss imbalance, asymmetric hopping, and complex potentials modify both electrical transport and quantum correlations. Using a family of generalized Su–Schrieffer–Heeger (SSH) superlattice Hamil-tonians with tunable sublattice asymmetry and non-reciprocity, we compute, linear response transport coefficients, and mixed-state entanglement measures across parameter regimes (PT-symmetric broken regime). We find robust signatures linking the onset of the skin effect and spectral winding to enhanced local currents and strongly inhomogeneous entanglement profiles, and topological edge modes to finite entanglement negativity in otherwise mixed steady states. Our results clarify when transport and entan…

item.title: Practical Evaluation of Quantum Kernel Methodsfor Radar Micro-Doppler Classification on Noisy Intermediate-Scale Quantum (NISQ) Hardware
item.description:
📚 Preprints
👤 Vikas Agnihotri Vikas Agnihotri, Jasleen Kaur Jasleen Kaur, Sarvagya Kaushik Sarvagya Kaushik
This work presents an empirical study of a Quantum Support Vector Machine(QSVM) for classification of radar micro-Doppler signatures, used here as astructured benchmark for evaluating quantum kernel methods under realistichardware constraints. Extracted classical features are reduced using PrincipalComponent Analysis (PCA) to enable compact quantum encoding, and mappedinto a quantum kernel feature space through a fully entangling ZZFeatureMap.Classification is performed using a kernel-based QSVM and compared with aclassical SVM baseline under consistent preprocessing and evaluation conditions.Performance is assessed in both ideal simulation and on NISQ-era superconducting quantum processors, specifically IBM Torino (133-qubit) and IBM Fez(156-qubit). Results show that the QSVM attains com…

item.title: [ASAP] Multiresolution Quantum Chemistry: Nonlinear Response Properties at the Basis Set Limit
item.description:
📚 ACS
👤 Adrian Hurtado, Hideo Sekino, and Robert J. Harrison
Journal of Chemical Theory and Computation DOI: 10.1021/acs.jctc.6c00466

item.title: Chemical diversity of bilin species defines the chiroptical landscape of phytochromes
item.description:
📚 Preprints
👤 Andreas Winkler, Oliver Eder, Chongyao Wei, Marie Reißenbüchel, Igor Schapiro
Phytochromes are widespread red/far-red light-sensing photoreceptors across several kingdoms of life that enable organisms to adapt their physiology to changing light environments. Major evolutionary transitions, from proteobacteria to cyanobacteria, algae and plants, imposed selective pressure on these proteins to diversify their spectral properties through changes in bilin cofactor preference. This adaptation coincided with a structural relocation of a cysteine residue, the covalent cofactor attachment site, from the N-terminal segment (NTS) to the GAF domain. Here, using the model protein IsPadC, we demonstrate how the shift from biliverdin- to phycocyanobilin-binding phytochromes modulates spectroscopic signatures while retaining overall functionality. We further resolve the long-stan…
